query: What is the current national norovirus category on the WastewaterSCAN dashboard as of today (High/Medium/Low), and is it rising, falling, or stable? Note the unusual summer 2026 GII.17 off-season surge.
answer:
## Summary

**Current national norovirus category on WastewaterSCAN (as of ~July 13, 2026): HIGH and RISING** — per the most recent (July) dashboard-based reporting, driven by the unusual off-season summer surge.

**Key nuance on trajectory:**
- The official **WastewaterSCAN June Update** (data through June 30) showed **MEDIUM / falling** (median concentration ~37% below May) — a brief trough.
- **July news coverage** (NBC News) citing the live dashboard has norovirus back to **HIGH / rising**, especially in the Northeast.
- Because categories use rolling ~21-day windows, the label has been volatile and flipped between Medium and High during this atypical summer wave.

**Summer 2026 off-season surge:** Confirmed — norovirus normally peaks in winter, but summer 2026 shows a warm-weather wave across 12+ states (led by Utah, Texas, Ohio, North Carolina), tied to crowds, restaurants/camps, cruise ships, and trail exposures.

**GII.17 context:** The variant that reshaped the picture — GII.17 surged from <10% of U.S. outbreaks (2022–23) to ~75% (2024–25), overtaking GII.4, and is associated with earlier/atypical season timing consistent with this off-season activity. (Note: reporting establishes GII.17 predominance as the backdrop; I did not find a source explicitly branding the summer 2026 surge as a discrete "GII.17 off-season event" by name.)

**Caveat:** The live dashboard is JavaScript-rendered and couldn't be scraped for today's exact value, so "High/rising" is the latest reported state at medium confidence.

sources.title: Nationwide WastewaterSCAN June 2026 Update
sources.snippet: Norovirus GII: medium concentrations and downward trend in last 21 days; June median ~37% lower than May; detected in 99% of samples.

sources.title: Vomiting virus is spreading across most of the U.S.
sources.snippet: At the national level, norovirus is still in the HIGH category due to high concentrations over the last 21 days; rates rising in the Northeast; GII.17 ~75% of outbreaks in 2024-25.

sources.title: Norovirus Summer 2026: Which States Are Seeing the Highest Activity?
sources.snippet: Unusual warm-weather summer 2026 surge; rising and approaching July peak; 12+ states elevated led by Utah, Texas, Ohio, North Carolina.

sources.title: Increasing Predominance of Norovirus GII.17 over GII.4, United States, 2022-2025
sources.snippet: GII.17 outbreaks rose from <10% in 2022-23 to 75% in 2024-25, surpassing GII.4; season started earlier.

sources.title: Norovirus Showing High Levels, New Data Reveals
sources.snippet: WastewaterSCAN dashboard viewed June 2 showed high norovirus levels nationally, including the Northeast.

sources.title: WastewaterSCAN Dashboard
sources.snippet: Live national dashboard for norovirus (Noro_G2) category and trend; JavaScript-rendered, values not scrapable via fetch.
